lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Project

> The speaker introduces themselves as Jose Hurtado Juarez, a student conducting an oral history interview with Antonia Saucedo as part of a collaborative project between the College of Idaho and the Idaho State Historical Society. The project aims to capture the experiences of individuals living in the Treasure Valley and is supported by the NEA Spotlight on Humanities Grant program.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Project
metadata.synopsis: The speaker introduces themselves as Jose Hurtado Juarez, a student conducting an oral history interview with Antonia Saucedo as part of a collaborative project between the College of Idaho and the Idaho State Historical Society. The project aims to capture the experiences of individuals living in the Treasure Valley and is supported by the NEA Spotlight on Humanities Grant program.

lines.line: # Journey from Mexico to the US and Initial Impressions

> The speaker describes their journey from Mexico to the US as relatively smooth and notes a quick adjustment to the change. The most striking first impression for the speaker was the sense of safety they felt upon arrival, contrasting with their experiences in Zacatecas, Mexico, which they describe as unsafe.

metadata.title: Journey from Mexico to the US and Initial Impressions
metadata.synopsis: The speaker describes their journey from Mexico to the US as relatively smooth and notes a quick adjustment to the change. The most striking first impression for the speaker was the sense of safety they felt upon arrival, contrasting with their experiences in Zacatecas, Mexico, which they describe as unsafe.

lines.line: # Population Growth and Business Impact

> The speaker reflects on the benefits and challenges brought about by Idaho's rapid population growth. While acknowledging that the growth has been beneficial for their business due to increased demand for housing, they also recognize the difficulties it has created for others, particularly in the housing market.

metadata.title: Population Growth and Business Impact
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the benefits and challenges brought about by Idaho's rapid population growth. While acknowledging that the growth has been beneficial for their business due to increased demand for housing, they also recognize the difficulties it has created for others, particularly in the housing market.

lines.line: # Choosing Idaho as Home and Family Adjustment

> The speaker chose to reside in Idaho because their brother was already established there, and they believed it was a good state to raise a family. They feel that their family has adjusted well to the move, and they appreciate the safety and outdoor activities that Idaho offers.

metadata.title: Choosing Idaho as Home and Family Adjustment
metadata.synopsis: The speaker chose to reside in Idaho because their brother was already established there, and they believed it was a good state to raise a family. They feel that their family has adjusted well to the move, and they appreciate the safety and outdoor activities that Idaho offers.

lines.line: # Family Life and Education in Idaho

> The speaker shares that their favorite part of Idaho is the mountains and the outdoor activities available. They would choose to raise their family in Idaho again despite some challenges their children faced in the education system. The speaker believes the education system in the United States, including Idaho, could be improved.

metadata.title: Family Life and Education in Idaho
metadata.synopsis: The speaker shares that their favorite part of Idaho is the mountains and the outdoor activities available. They would choose to raise their family in Idaho again despite some challenges their children faced in the education system. The speaker believes the education system in the United States, including Idaho, could be improved.

lines.line: # Cultural Divides and School Experiences

> The speaker discusses the cultural divide in schools, particularly between Latino and Caucasian students, which prompted their move from Nampa to Meridian. They express concern about the division and the challenges it posed for their son, who was caught between identifying with a specific group.

metadata.title: Cultural Divides and School Experiences
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the cultural divide in schools, particularly between Latino and Caucasian students, which prompted their move from Nampa to Meridian. They express concern about the division and the challenges it posed for their son, who was caught between identifying with a specific group.

lines.line: # Relocating for Better Education and Business Considerations

> The speaker explains that the primary reason for moving from Nampa to Meridian was the pursuit of better educational opportunities for their children. They also touch on how the move did not affect their roofing business, which operates throughout the Treasure Valley.

metadata.title: Relocating for Better Education and Business Considerations
metadata.synopsis: The speaker explains that the primary reason for moving from Nampa to Meridian was the pursuit of better educational opportunities for their children. They also touch on how the move did not affect their roofing business, which operates throughout the Treasure Valley.

lines.line: # Starting a Business During Economic Downturn

> The speaker recounts the founding of their roofing company during the economic downturn of 2008. Despite the challenging economic climate, the business managed to survive and grow, which the speaker attributes to doing something right, as indicated by a client's comment.

metadata.title: Starting a Business During Economic Downturn
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts the founding of their roofing company during the economic downturn of 2008. Despite the challenging economic climate, the business managed to survive and grow, which the speaker attributes to doing something right, as indicated by a client's comment.

lines.line: # Work History and Educational Background

> The speaker provides an overview of their work history, starting from a young age with goals set for their future. They discuss their progression from housekeeping to managing a cleaning company and eventually starting their own business. The speaker also mentions the lack of formal education but emphasizes their determination and success in business.

metadata.title: Work History and Educational Background
metadata.synopsis: The speaker provides an overview of their work history, starting from a young age with goals set for their future. They discuss their progression from housekeeping to managing a cleaning company and eventually starting their own business. The speaker also mentions the lack of formal education but emphasizes their determination and success in business.

lines.line: # Sense of Community and Belonging

> The speaker felt most welcomed in Nampa due to its larger Latino population and the sense of community there. They share an incident in their current neighborhood that made them feel out of place, highlighting the challenges of feeling welcome in different areas of Idaho.

metadata.title: Sense of Community and Belonging
metadata.synopsis: The speaker felt most welcomed in Nampa due to its larger Latino population and the sense of community there. They share an incident in their current neighborhood that made them feel out of place, highlighting the challenges of feeling welcome in different areas of Idaho.

lines.line: # Cultural Heritage and Family Activities

> The speaker discusses the limited opportunities to celebrate their Mexican heritage in the Treasure Valley, mentioning only the Cinco de Mayo celebrations. They also share the various outdoor activities their family enjoys, such as camping, fishing, and playing volleyball.

metadata.title: Cultural Heritage and Family Activities
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the limited opportunities to celebrate their Mexican heritage in the Treasure Valley, mentioning only the Cinco de Mayo celebrations. They also share the various outdoor activities their family enjoys, such as camping, fishing, and playing volleyball.

lines.line: # The Role of Religion in Community

> The speaker emphasizes the importance of religion in their life, particularly the sense of community it brings. They attend a church in Boise that draws people from various parts of Idaho due to its welcoming leader, and they describe the church as a unifying force for the Latino community.

metadata.title: The Role of Religion in Community
metadata.synopsis: The speaker emphasizes the importance of religion in their life, particularly the sense of community it brings. They attend a church in Boise that draws people from various parts of Idaho due to its welcoming leader, and they describe the church as a unifying force for the Latino community.

lines.line: # Engagement with Idaho Politics

> The speaker discusses their engagement with Idaho politics, expressing a nuanced view that straddles liberal and conservative ideologies. They share experiences of feeling unwelcome due to political messages in their community and concerns about the state's readiness for further population growth.

metadata.title: Engagement with Idaho Politics
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses their engagement with Idaho politics, expressing a nuanced view that straddles liberal and conservative ideologies. They share experiences of feeling unwelcome due to political messages in their community and concerns about the state's readiness for further population growth.

lines.line: # Perceptions of Idaho and Self-Identification

> The speaker talks about the perception of Idaho as uneducated and resistant to change, as well as the reasons people move to the state. They share the moment they began to identify as an Idahoan, which was tied to their contributions to the state through taxes and raising successful children.

metadata.title: Perceptions of Idaho and Self-Identification
metadata.synopsis: The speaker talks about the perception of Idaho as uneducated and resistant to change, as well as the reasons people move to the state. They share the moment they began to identify as an Idahoan, which was tied to their contributions to the state through taxes and raising successful children.

lines.line: # Advocating for Children with Special Needs

> The speaker expresses a desire for more support and resources for children with special needs in Idaho. They highlight the challenges faced by parents in finding appropriate programs and schools, and the need for better preparation and assistance within the educational system.

metadata.title: Advocating for Children with Special Needs
metadata.synopsis: The speaker expresses a desire for more support and resources for children with special needs in Idaho. They highlight the challenges faced by parents in finding appropriate programs and schools, and the need for better preparation and assistance within the educational system.

> The speaker introduces themselves as Jose Hurtado Juarez, a student conducting an oral history interview with Antonia Saucedo as part of a collaborative project between the College of Idaho and the Idaho State Historical Society. The project aims to capture the experiences of individuals living in the Treasure Valley and is supported by the NEA Spotlight on Humanities Grant program.
# Journey from Mexico to the US and Initial Impressions

> The speaker describes their journey from Mexico to the US as relatively smooth and notes a quick adjustment to the change. The most striking first impression for the speaker was the sense of safety they felt upon arrival, contrasting with their experiences in Zacatecas, Mexico, which they describe as unsafe.
# Population Growth and Business Impact

> The speaker reflects on the benefits and challenges brought about by Idaho's rapid population growth. While acknowledging that the growth has been beneficial for their business due to increased demand for housing, they also recognize the difficulties it has created for others, particularly in the housing market.
# Choosing Idaho as Home and Family Adjustment

> The speaker chose to reside in Idaho because their brother was already established there, and they believed it was a good state to raise a family. They feel that their family has adjusted well to the move, and they appreciate the safety and outdoor activities that Idaho offers.
# Family Life and Education in Idaho

> The speaker shares that their favorite part of Idaho is the mountains and the outdoor activities available. They would choose to raise their family in Idaho again despite some challenges their children faced in the education system. The speaker believes the education system in the United States, including Idaho, could be improved.
# Cultural Divides and School Experiences

> The speaker discusses the cultural divide in schools, particularly between Latino and Caucasian students, which prompted their move from Nampa to Meridian. They express concern about the division and the challenges it posed for their son, who was caught between identifying with a specific group.
# Relocating for Better Education and Business Considerations

> The speaker explains that the primary reason for moving from Nampa to Meridian was the pursuit of better educational opportunities for their children. They also touch on how the move did not affect their roofing business, which operates throughout the Treasure Valley.
# Starting a Business During Economic Downturn

> The speaker recounts the founding of their roofing company during the economic downturn of 2008. Despite the challenging economic climate, the business managed to survive and grow, which the speaker attributes to doing something right, as indicated by a client's comment.
# Work History and Educational Background

> The speaker provides an overview of their work history, starting from a young age with goals set for their future. They discuss their progression from housekeeping to managing a cleaning company and eventually starting their own business. The speaker also mentions the lack of formal education but emphasizes their determination and success in business.
# Sense of Community and Belonging

> The speaker felt most welcomed in Nampa due to its larger Latino population and the sense of community there. They share an incident in their current neighborhood that made them feel out of place, highlighting the challenges of feeling welcome in different areas of Idaho.
# Cultural Heritage and Family Activities

> The speaker discusses the limited opportunities to celebrate their Mexican heritage in the Treasure Valley, mentioning only the Cinco de Mayo celebrations. They also share the various outdoor activities their family enjoys, such as camping, fishing, and playing volleyball.
# The Role of Religion in Community

> The speaker emphasizes the importance of religion in their life, particularly the sense of community it brings. They attend a church in Boise that draws people from various parts of Idaho due to its welcoming leader, and they describe the church as a unifying force for the Latino community.
# Engagement with Idaho Politics

> The speaker discusses their engagement with Idaho politics, expressing a nuanced view that straddles liberal and conservative ideologies. They share experiences of feeling unwelcome due to political messages in their community and concerns about the state's readiness for further population growth.
# Perceptions of Idaho and Self-Identification

> The speaker talks about the perception of Idaho as uneducated and resistant to change, as well as the reasons people move to the state. They share the moment they began to identify as an Idahoan, which was tied to their contributions to the state through taxes and raising successful children.
# Advocating for Children with Special Needs

> The speaker expresses a desire for more support and resources for children with special needs in Idaho. They highlight the challenges faced by parents in finding appropriate programs and schools, and the need for better preparation and assistance within the educational system.

story.title: Saucedo_Antonia_OH2822 Hist305
story.description: In this oral history interview, Antonia Saucedo, who immigrated from Mexico, shares her experiences of living in Idaho's Treasure Valley. Key topics include her sense of safety and belonging in the U.S., challenges with education for her children, and the cultural divide she observed in schools, particularly between Latino and Caucasian students. She reflects on her journey to starting a successful business and the importance of maintaining cultural heritage. Additionally, Antonia discusses the role of religion in her community, the complexities of Idaho politics, and the particular challenges faced by women and minority groups. She also highlights the lack of support for children with special needs and appreciates Idaho's natural beauty, emphasizing outdoor family activities.
